title: cyperus laetus subsp. oostachyus
intro: Cyperus laetus subsp. oostachyus, also known as the yellow papyrus or water sedge, is a perennial aquatic plant native to tropical Africa. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in treating various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The yellow papyrus has also been used to treat wounds, as its extracts have been found to promote wound healing and prevent infection. In herbal preparations, Cyperus laetus subsp. oostachyus is often used in teas, infusions, and poultices to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health complaints.
